title: ML prediction, reality or madness?
author: Iaconisii
selftext: I have a strategy that performs perfectly in backtest but, unfortunately, I realized that it takes the future ema and then performs the calculations on data that, in real time, I don't have. Any advice on how to try to predict future ema? (I had thought about ML but, not understanding much, I have no idea how to start and how to structure everything so that it is functional and optimized)

comments.author: Impossible-Rent-2112
comments.body: This is classic target/data leakage… using a future EMA (or any look-ahead feature) will make the backtest look like a dream but fall apart live. I’ve run into it before the fix for me was...recalculate EMAs so they only use past bars (shift them by +1 bar)....add a quick check in code to make sure no feature at time *t* is using info from *t+1....t*est again with walk-forward or expanding-window validation..If the equity curve still looks good after that, you probably have something real. If it tanks, it was just leakage.
